testcases_summary: The tests verify the functionality of the behavior metrics API, covering scenarios such as successful retrieval and creation of metrics, handling of SQLAlchemy errors during these operations, and cases where metrics creation fails or input payload is missing.

The tests verify the functionality of the sales performance tracking endpoints for daily, weekly, and monthly sales. The main scenarios covered include successful data retrieval, handling of database errors, and validation of input parameters. The overall purpose of the tests is to ensure the endpoints return appropriate responses for various success and failure conditions.

The tests verify that API endpoints for retrieving user session duration, page views, and click-through rates return the correct data and handle database errors gracefully by returning a 500 status code.

The test code verifies the creation and handling of BehaviorMetric objects in a database, covering scenarios including: creation with full values, minimum default values, edge values, and high values. It also ensures proper handling of invalid user IDs, default timestamps, and automatic timestamp updates on entity modification.

The tests verify the creation of valid sales records and check for integrity errors when mandatory fields are null (date, amount, category, product_id). They also test boundary conditions for the amount field, including zero, negative, and very large values.
The tests verify the creation and data integrity of UserSession, PageView, and ClickThroughRate objects, ensuring correct attribute assignments and handling of invalid data types.

The tests verify the functionality of the BehaviorMetricRepository class, including retrieving metrics by user ID, creating metrics with valid inputs, handling invalid input scenarios, and working with boundary values.

The tests verify the functionality of the SalesRepository for retrieving daily, weekly, and monthly sales data from a database. They cover scenarios with expected sales data and cases where no results are returned.

The tests verify the functionality of the UserBehaviorRepository class, specifically for calculating average session duration, total page views, and click-through rates. Main scenarios include positive cases, handling empty result sets, and managing exceptions during database queries.

The tests check if the behavior blueprint is registered successfully, validate that an invalid route returns a 404 status, test the response status of the root endpoint, assert the availability of specific routes within the blueprint, and verify the status response of a POST request to a defined route.

The tests verify the functionality of the BehaviorMetricService. It covers scenarios for retrieving metrics by user ID with and without existing metrics, successfully creating a new metric, and handling exceptions when metric creation fails.
The tests verify the SalesService class methods for retrieving daily, weekly, and monthly sales, covering successful data retrieval, empty results, and handling invalid date inputs.
